title: pectis purpurea var. lancifolia
intro: Pectis purpurea var. lancifolia, also known as lanceleaf pectis, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The lanceleaf pectis has been found to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing skin irritations. It can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
